title: Gradual migration across 3 services (phased plan required, rename + type change)
description: The fee column in the transactions table is renamed to processing_fee and its type changes from INT to BIGINT. There is no API change. Three downstream services consume the fee column: the Payments Service, the Reporting Service, and the Reconciliation Service. Each must be migrated independently in a phased plan to avoid data loss or downtime. Phase 1 adds processing_fee alongside fee (expand). Phase 2 dual-writes to both columns and migrates each service to read from processing_fee. Phase 3 removes the fee column once all three services have been migrated and verified (contract). The type change from INT to BIGINT is a widening change and is database-safe, but the rename is breaking for all three services.

schema_changes.reason: Removing the fee column breaks all three downstream services (Payments, Reporting, Reconciliation) that currently read from or write to this column.
Adding the processing_fee column is a safe additive change. Existing INSERT statements that omit processing_fee will continue to work since the column is nullable.

Beyond the rename, the type changes from INT to BIGINT. While BIGINT is a widening change at the DB level, combined with the rename it constitutes a breaking change for any service that reads fee as an Int type and would now receive BIGINT values via processing_fee.

migration_phases.name: Expand
migration_phases.description: Add processing_fee column alongside fee. Begin dual-writing to both columns in the application layer.

migration_phases.name: Migrate
migration_phases.description: Update all three services (Payments, Reporting, Reconciliation) to read from processing_fee instead of fee. Backfill processing_fee for all existing rows using fee values.

migration_phases.name: Contract
migration_phases.description: After all three services are verified reading processing_fee, stop writing to fee and drop the fee column.
